commit.message:
feat: per-model passthrough routing via PASSTHROUGH_MODELS

Replace global PROXY_MODE with PASSTHROUGH_MODELS config. Routes are
now decided per-request based on model prefix matching:
- Models matching configured prefixes → Anthropic passthrough (/messages)
- All other models → OpenAI conversion (/chat/completions)

This allows a single proxy to serve mixed backends (e.g., GLM via
OpenAI format + MiniMax via Anthropic format on the same endpoint).

Example: PASSTHROUGH_MODELS="minimax" routes minimax-* models to
Anthropic passthrough while glm-* models go through OpenAI conversion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add README, CI/publish workflows, and project URLs

Add comprehensive README documenting uvx-based usage — no installation
required, just `uvx --from git+https://github.com/amfaro/jarify.git jarify`.

Covers all CLI commands (`fmt`, `lint`, `init`, `show-config`), the full
formatting style guide (leading commas, CTE layout, keyword/function casing,
IS NOT NULL, NULLS suppression), lint rules table, and dev workflow.

Add two GitHub Actions workflows:
- `ci.yml` — runs `mise run lint` and `mise run test` on push/PR to main
  using `blacksmith-2vcpu-ubuntu-2404`
- `publish.yml` — fires on `v*` tags; builds wheel + sdist with `uv build`
  then creates a GitHub Release with artifacts attached using
  `amfaro-github-workflows-managment` app token (contents:write)

Note: GitHub Packages does not support a PyPI-compatible registry for
Python. Distributions are published as GitHub Release assets instead.
Users install via the git URL (no auth needed, public repo) or directly
from a release wheel URL.

Add `[project.urls]` to `pyproject.toml`.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(generator): implement company-style formatting with leading commas and CTE style

Overhaul the generator to match the team's opinionated SQL style:

- Leading-comma style for SELECT, ORDER BY, and function args:
  first item gets one extra space so content aligns with `,item` lines
- Override `format_args` so multi-line function calls (e.g. `read_parquet`)
  also use leading commas, not trailing
- CTE opening paren on its own line (`alias AS\n(`) with comma-prefix
  separator between CTEs (`,name AS\n(`)
- Set `dialect='duckdb'` on the generator so default NULLS ordering is not
  re-emitted for every ORDER BY / window spec
- Set `normalize_functions='lower'` so DuckDB functions stay lowercase
  (`read_parquet`, `glob`, `getvariable`, etc.)
- Override `not_sql` to preserve `IS NOT NULL` instead of rewriting to
  `NOT x IS NULL`

Change defaults in `JarifyConfig`: `leading_commas=True`, `trailing_commas=False`

Add `complex/real_world` fixture built from a real production query using
five CTEs, SEMI JOINs, multi-condition JOINs, `read_parquet` with
`hive_partitioning` / `hive_types`, CASE WHEN IS NOT NULL, and ORDER BY.

Regenerate all existing fixture expected files to match the new style.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(sdk): namespace improvements for deepagents (#2386)

# Namespace factory refactor

This PR changes `StoreBackend`'s `namespace` factory to receive a
`Runtime` instance directly instead of a `BackendContext` wrapper.
Factories now look like `lambda rt: (rt.server_info.user.identity,
"fs")` rather than `lambda ctx: (ctx.runtime.context.user_id, "fs")`.

Backwards compatibility is preserved via `_NamespaceRuntimeCompat`,
which duck-types as both `Runtime` and the legacy `BackendContext` and
emits `DeprecationWarning`s when `.runtime` or `.state` is accessed.
`BackendContext` and the old-style callable signature are deprecated and
will be removed in v0.7. Tests across the backends and middleware suites
were updated to the new signature.

feat(auth): add Express middleware for JWT auth, disable Wonderwall autoLogin

Replace Wonderwall autoLogin with Express-level JWT middleware to allow
M2M traffic from KISS while enforcing auth on all other routes.

Middleware behavior:
- Health checks (/api/isalive, /api/isready): pass through
- M2M routes (/api/v1/*): pass through (own introspection-based auth)
- Valid user JWT: pass through (validates signature, issuer, audience, expiry)
- App tokens (idtyp=app): rejected on non-M2M routes (defense-in-depth)
- Browser requests without token: redirect to /oauth2/login
- API requests without token: 401 Unauthorized
- Development without NAIS cluster: pass through

NAIS config: autoLogin: true → false

121 new tests: 23 middleware unit tests + 98 per-endpoint enforcement tests.
Reviewed by GPT 5.4, Codex, Sonnet, and Opus (2 rounds).

feat: implement phases 3, 5, and 6 of MVP plan

Phase 3 — DuckDB-specific lint rules:
- `duckdb-type-style`: flags non-canonical DuckDB type names that survive
  sqlglot parsing (FLOAT → prefer REAL, NVARCHAR → prefer TEXT)
- `duckdb-prefer-qualify`: flags subquery-based window function filtering
  where a QUALIFY clause would be cleaner
- New fixtures: duckdb/qualify (QUALIFY clause) and duckdb/pivot (PIVOT syntax)

Phase 5 — Config and rule toggle system:
- All lint rules now have individual severity fields on JarifyConfig
- `jarify init` generates a starter jarify.toml with documented defaults
- `jarify show-config` displays the resolved config as syntax-highlighted TOML
- Config fields are discovered via dataclass introspection; unknown keys in
  jarify.toml are silently ignored for forward compatibility

Phase 6 — CI integration and diff output:
- `jarify fmt --diff` prints a syntax-highlighted unified diff instead of
  writing files; non-zero exit when diffs exist
- `jarify fmt -` and `jarify lint -` read from stdin (label via --stdin-filename)
- `jarify lint` colors error-severity violations red, warn yellow
- Exit codes: 0 = clean, 1 = violations/changes, 2 = parse/IO error

39 tests passing.
